extra_model_fields.notes: Priority is to fix the numeric/labeling inconsistency and to provide explicit, machine-checkable traceability. If time-constrained, at minimum provide plain-text formula appendix and a CSV export of computed summary tables.

Peer review of appendix cell mappings.

priority_items: ri_1: artifact traceability / programmatic verifiability (workbook parse limitation)
ri_2: technical correctness (minor numeric/label inconsistency for O1)
ri_4: recommendation operationalization (sensitivity and validation-burden quantification)

failed_rubric_items: ri_1: traceability/programmatic verification (workbook parseability and explicit cell references needed)
ri_2: minor technical correctness labeling (O1 residual improvement sign/percent)

planned_changes.description: Correct the O1 'Residual Improvement' sign and percentage in expert_memo.md and verify the workbook formula that computes post-avg residual for O1. Update both memo and workbook so numbers and labels match.
verification_steps: Recompute baseline avg proxy residual and O1 post-avg residual in workbook and confirm percent change matches memo.
Include the exact formula used for percent-change (e.g., (post - baseline)/baseline) as plain text in memo appendix.

planned_changes.description: Add a plain-text traceability appendix to expert_memo.md listing sheet names and exact cell addresses for all key diagnostics and summary table entries, and include the exact formulas used in those cells.
verification_steps: Append 'Workbook Traceability Appendix' with mappings like: Diagnostics!B2 = call_mid (row R1), Diagnostics!C2 = put_mid (row R1), Diagnostics!D2 = ABS(B2-C2) etc.
Ensure each summary table value in the memo references a workbook cell (sheet!cell).

planned_changes.description: Make the workbook fully parseable by common tooling (remove macros/protection, avoid nonstandard objects) and/or include a CSV/plain-text export of computed summary tables and a sheet with formulas exported as text.
verification_steps: Test openpyxl (or equivalent) can open the workbook and read the formula-text sheet; if not possible, include formulas-as-text worksheet and CSV exports of computed tables.

planned_changes.description: Add a 'Sensitivity Scenarios' worksheet that enumerates small scenario variations (e.g., +/-5%, +/-10% on option side adjustments) and recomputes costs/residuals, and add a 'Validation Burden' table estimating expert-hours and required tests for implementing O2.
verification_steps: Include at least 3 enumerated scenarios with recomputed post-avg residuals and budget checks.
Include validation-burden rows: estimated expert-hours, test types, and approximate calendar days required.

planned_changes.description: After numeric fixes and added sensitivity detail, update the recommendation paragraph to reference the new appendix cells and scenarios and confirm whether O2 remains preferred under enumerated sensitivities.
verification_steps: Run the sensitivity scenarios and confirm recommendation rank order is consistent; document any conditions under which ranking would change.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Resolve a minor numeric/label inconsistency in the memo, improve spreadsheet traceability and programmatic verifiability, and strengthen sensitivity/validation-burden evidence to increase operational readiness.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Good deliverables and clear conservative methodology. Main issues: (1) internal numeric/labeling inconsistency in the memo option table for O1 (sign mismatch between Post Avg Residual and Residual Improvement); (2) inability to programmatically verify in-sheet formulas and row-level citations because the preview tool reported openpyxl unavailable; (3) sensitivity checks and validation-burden discussion are qualitative rather than enumerated and quantified.
